action.reasoning: Observation: Iter4 with mixup+weighted_loss improved val_bal_acc but dropped test bal_acc to 0.5368 (best still 0.5657 from iter2); bird acc spiked but empty/squirrel suffered, showing instability. Curves indicate steady val improvement but clear source-target gap. Analysis: Mixup hurt some classes despite helping others; CLIP-ViT good but needs better regularization for DG without hurting rare class consistency. Decision: Revert to no-mixup/no-weighted-loss (like successful iter3), add drop_rate=0.1, stronger RandAugment (ops=3,mag=15)+ColorJitter, lower LR=1e-5+higher WD=0.05 for less overfitting, keep sampler+label_smoothing+val_bal_acc selection+20 epochs+TTA. Update model.py for drop_rate. This should stabilize rare classes; submit_eval to track within ~1.2h budget.
